skill.name: general-video
skill.description: Fallback workflow for planning, building, validating, and handing off custom HyperFrames video compositions when no specialized video skill fits the request.
skill.summary: Author custom HyperFrames video compositions using a structured design, layout, animation, and validation workflow.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are Markdown inline-code references, headings, examples, and skill routing text rather than executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The skill does instruct agents to run HyperFrames CLI commands and a local media script, so publication is reasonable with an external-command warning.

medium_findings.title: Legitimate External Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to run a local media generation script and HyperFrames CLI checks. These commands are relevant to video rendering, but they execute local or package-provided code and should be reviewed in untrusted workspaces.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed lines contain explicit shell command examples for node and npx. The commands match the skill purpose, so the risk is execution exposure rather than malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Matches Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings point to Markdown inline code, fenced examples, route names, and reference paths. I found no Ruby code or dynamic shell interpolation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is contained in a Markdown skill file, and the flagged backticks delimit names, paths, and examples. There is no executable Ruby context.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary Markdown text such as .md filenames, headings, or words inside documentation. No cryptographic API, hash implementation, or password handling code is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source file is prose documentation and contains no cryptographic operations. The static labels do not correspond to security-relevant code.

content.value_statement: Teams need a reliable path for custom videos that do not fit product, website, explainer, PR, caption, recut, or motion graphic workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through design, planning, HTML composition, audio handling, validation, and handoff.
actual_capabilities: Routes custom video requests when specialized HyperFrames workflows do not fit.
Guides discovery for audience, platform, priorities, and variation needs.
Requires a design-system step before choosing colors, typography, or layout.
Defines a layout-first process before adding GSAP animation timelines.
Maps common video intents to HyperFrames creative, animation, media, and core references.
Requires lint, validate, inspect, and final preview handoff checks.
limitations: It is a fallback workflow, not the preferred path for clear product, website, explainer, PR, caption, recut, or short motion requests.
It does not perform NLE-style editing of completed footage.
It excludes live or render-time data sources for deterministic rendering.
Audio generation depends on the separate HyperFrames media workflow and available credentials or local fallbacks.

use_cases.title: Build a Brand Sizzle Reel
use_cases.description: Create a multi-scene HTML video with design direction, pacing, transitions, and validation checks.

use_cases.title: Author a Custom Title Sequence
use_cases.description: Turn a precise title card or intro request into a scoped HyperFrames composition without unnecessary extra scenes.

use_cases.title: Produce a Video Prototype
use_cases.description: Explore audience, platform, visual identity, rhythm, layout, and motion before rendering an MP4.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Custom Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small composition.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the general-video workflow to create a 10-second title card for our event. Keep it focused on the title, date, and one visual motif.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Scene Brand Piece
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and platform context.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use general-video to make a 45-second brand reel for a developer audience on our website hero. Plan the structure, visual identity, rhythm, layout, and validation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audio-Supported Composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that includes narration and music.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use general-video for a narrated 60-second internal launch video. Include a neutral audio request, caption placement, scene timing, and final HyperFrames validation.

prompt_templates.title: Custom Direction With Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with strict scope and quality gates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use general-video to create a custom 90-second montage from this brief. Ask only essential discovery questions, follow our design spec, build layout before animation, and do not render MP4 unless I approve.

output_examples.input: A request for a short launch intro that does not match the product launch workflow.
output_examples.output: A scoped plan with a visual concept, scene structure, timing notes, static layout direction, animation references, and validation checklist.

output_examples.input: A vague request for a custom brand video for executives.
output_examples.output: A discovery-driven direction covering audience, platform, priority, variation options, design-system source, and next build steps.

output_examples.input: A multi-scene narrated video request with music and captions.
output_examples.output: A production plan that routes audio through the shared media engine, mounts tracks, places captions, and validates the HyperFrames composition.

best_practices: Use this skill only after confirming no specialized HyperFrames workflow clearly fits the request.
Build the most visible static layout before adding GSAP motion or transitions.
Run HyperFrames lint, validate, and inspect checks before final handoff.
anti_patterns: Do not silently add extra scenes, music, captions, or effects beyond the requested scope.
Do not invent colors or fonts when a design spec or required creative reference exists.
Do not use live data, render-time network fetches, or non-deterministic behavior in compositions.

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use it for custom HyperFrames videos when product, website, explainer, PR, caption, recut, motion graphic, or Remotion workflows do not fit.

faq.question: Can it create long videos?
faq.answer: Yes. It is input and length agnostic, but longer work still needs planning, layout, animation, and validation discipline.

faq.question: Does it edit existing finished footage?
faq.answer: No. Use embedded captions or talking-head recut workflows for existing footage, depending on the desired output.

faq.question: Does it support audio?
faq.answer: Yes, when the request needs audio. It routes narration, music, sound effects, and captions through HyperFrames media guidance.

faq.question: What does it produce?
faq.answer: It helps an agent create and validate HyperFrames HTML compositions, then hand off a preview or render when requested.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a warning. It contains legitimate command instructions, so users should run them only in trusted project environments.
